title: FileChunk.data has no size cap at deserialization
description: messages.rs: ClearMessage::FileChunk.data is Vec<u8> with no size limit enforced at serde deserialization. A hostile peer inside an MLS group can send a FileChunk with data containing hundreds of MB, causing the allocator to run before any application-layer check. The CLI checks size_bytes > 10 MB in commands.rs, but that guard runs AFTER serde_json::from_slice has already allocated the Vec. The daemon relay.rs also deserializes ClearMessage at line 130 with no pre-check. Fix: add a deserialize_chunk_data function similar to deserialize_total_chunks, capping data at e.g. 48*1024 bytes (consistent with the CLI's 45 KB chunk size).
